_note: Outdoor feed: ski Dec-Apr, backpack May-Oct. Western US focus. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: Best Hikes in New Mexico: 8 Scenic Trails for Beginners
items.snark: La Luz Trail in the Sandias gets the climb right with serious elevation and that desert to pine transition. Chase elevation, not mileage, and you'll get the real New Mexico.

items.headline: The Best Ultralight Backpacking Gear You Can Buy in 2026
items.snark: Alpha Direct material lives up to the hype for warmth and breathability, but trekking pole tents and 7-ounce rain shells that survived full downpours are the real MVPs here.

items.headline: Peg Leg on Dangerous Encounters Over 22,000+ Miles of Thru-Hiking
items.snark: First woman to do the border to border calendar year triple crown, then kept going to log 10,070 miles in 2025. The dangerous encounters story is just a warmup.

items.headline: Welcome, PCT Class of 2026
items.snark: Light snow year in the Sierra means Kennedy Meadows timing is looking good, but the desert rules never change. Start early, rest midday, carry more water than you think.

items.headline: Trail Days 2026 In Review: Experiencing the CDT Community at Its Best
items.snark: Silver City Trail Days brought Hiker Olympics, pie eating contests, and that energy from this year's northbound CDT class. Community at its absolute best in New Mexico.

items.headline: 2026 Hiking Gear Trends, And Whether I Think They're Actually Worth It
items.snark: Battery powered sleeping pad pumps are actually worth it because hyperventilating into your pad after a 30 mile day is miserable. Garmin watches for battery life alone, skip the vintage cameras.

items.headline: The Gear That Backpacker's Editors Loved in March 2026
items.snark: Luxefly freeze dried burrata in a chile relleno is genuinely impressive trail food. Patagonia's free repair program saved $400 on snow bibs and they're still crushing it all season.

items.headline: Northern New Mexico Hiking Recommendations for Every Season of 2026
items.snark: Frijoles Canyon Trail past Alcove House goes deep into Bandelier Wilderness with solitude and beaver habitats recovering from the Las Conchas Fire. Skip the crowds, go upstream.

items.headline: Colorado Trail Thru-hiking and Backpacking Guide
items.snark: 485 miles Denver to Durango with epic San Juan sections above 11,000 feet. Camp almost anywhere, no advance permits, smooth singletrack that gets rugged when it counts.

items.headline: CDT Hiker Starting Early Due to Record Low Snow in San Juans
items.snark: Snowpack in the San Juans so low it's silly, so this CDT hiker moved his start from May 1 to April 15. Record low snow years change the whole game in Colorado.
